items.rationale: Sees blockchains/tokens as the only viable way to restore the internet as an open, user-owned platform that shifts power from Big Tech back to creators and users. This is his foundational thesis and the subject of his book.
items.age_note: Core thesis stated repeatedly 2023-06 through 2024; most recently reaffirmed via book launch and ongoing capital deployment in 2024.

citations.quote: I explain why crypto/web3 is the only plausible path to sustaining the original vision of the internet as an open platform
I wrote RWO to help more people appreciate the potential of blockchains for building a better internet

items.rationale: Believes generative AI is breaking the creator-platform covenant; on-chain IP registration (Story Protocol) can let creators track, license, and get compensated for work used by AI. His most recent and active conviction bet.
items.age_note: Most recent major position — led $80M Series B in PIP Labs/Story on 2024-08-21.

citations.quote: We want to help Story become the infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.
creators need a modern way to ensure they get compensated for their work

items.rationale: Excited about games where players own assets and anyone can build on top of another's game (modding from background to foreground), enabling composability, interoperability, and permanence.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-09-21 (Proof of Play seed); not restated in 2024 corpus but consistent with his broader thesis.

citations.quote: Excited to announce that we're leading a seed investment in @ProofOfPlay, an on-chain game studio and tech company
players own their assets, and anyone can create their own game on top of another

items.rationale: Personally uses Farcaster and prefers its community quality over Twitter/X — a live signal of conviction in user-owned social platforms.
items.age_note: Stated 2024-01-18; recent and based on personal usage.

citations.quote: I use Farcaster all the time. The community is far better than on Twitter.

items.rationale: Endorses decentralized, open AI models as a US competitive advantage; opposes closed-only models that concentrate power in a few big companies. Aligns with his 'little tech vs big tech' framing.
items.age_note: Expressed via approving share of Zuckerberg's open-source essay on 2024-07-23; an endorsement rather than original framing.

citations.quote: The United States' advantage is decentralized and open innovation.

items.rationale: Expects VR to follow prior computing-platform cost curves (cheaper, faster, lighter) once Meta and Apple invest at scale.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2023-06-05 around Vision Pro launch; never restated — treat as aged and peripheral to his core crypto focus.

citations.quote: Excited for Vision Pro. With Meta and now Apple investment, VR will follow the same pattern as past computing platforms

items.rationale: Believes the current US approach perversely lets unproductive tokens thrive while productive companies/tokens face hurdles, limits innovation/privacy, and risks pushing builders offshore. Drives his heavy policy/PAC engagement.
items.age_note: Long-running concern (2023) intensifying through 2024 — FT op-ed 2024-04-18, FIT21 advocacy 2024-05, SAB121 veto disappointment 2024-06.

citations.quote: the backward incentives of a regulatory regime in the US that lets only memecoins thrive — while companies and tokens with more productive uses face hurdles
It's more clear than ever that we need to get comprehensive crypto legislation passed into law this year.

items.rationale: Views the speculative, casino-like culture around crypto as harmful — it damages public perception and undermines the technology's productive potential. Wants utility-driven, not speculation-driven, narratives.
items.age_note: Persistent stance from 2023-06 through 2024; restated in 2024-01 book framing.

citations.quote: stamping out the harmful "casino" culture that has developed around crypto
these technologies can support productive and not just speculative use cases

items.rationale: Concerned AI search and AI-generated content sever the creator-platform covenant — AI trained on human work without attribution or compensation removes the incentive to publish original content openly.
items.age_note: Most recent concern, articulated 2024-08-21 as motivation for the Story investment.

citations.quote: Advances in generative AI are likely to break this covenant.
If there's no attribution or compensation, what incentive will there be to publish original creations on the open internet?

items.rationale: Worries that a closed-model world concentrates power in a few big companies (and adversaries), shutting out startups, universities, and small business — consistent with his 'little tech vs big tech' worldview.
items.age_note: Expressed via endorsed Zuckerberg quote 2024-07-23; aligned with longstanding Big-Tech-vs-little-tech framing.

citations.quote: a world of only closed models results in a small number of big companies plus our geopolitical adversaries having access to leading models

items.rationale: Not opposed to memecoins per se, but uses them as evidence of broken regulatory incentives — they are the only tokens that thrive in the US while productive tokens are blocked. Emblematic of the casino culture he dislikes.
items.age_note: Stated 2024-04-18; he explicitly notes his aim is neither to defend nor diminish memecoins but to flag regulatory distortion.

citations.quote: My goal isn't to defend or to diminish memecoins.
a regulatory regime in the US that lets only memecoins thrive

risk_regime.rationale: Despite a hostile US regulatory backdrop, Dixon remains a committed long-term, structural bull on crypto/web3 — actively deploying capital (e.g., $80M into PIP Labs/Story, expanding to the UK, $47M total to Fairshake) and publishing a manifesto for the space. His concerns are about conditions and culture, not the underlying thesis; his posture is build-through-the-cycle conviction, not retreat.

notes: Dixon's view is overwhelmingly sector/thesis-level rather than token-level — he names almost no specific tokens to be bullish on, so tokens_excited is empty. His most RECENT and active conviction (2024-08) is the AI×crypto creator-IP intersection (Story Protocol), which now sits alongside his enduring open-internet thesis. Regulation is his dominant concern and consumes most of his 2024 public energy (FIT21, Fairshake, SAB121, FT op-ed), but it reads as frustration with friction rather than loss of conviction. Conflicts were minimal; where stances are old and unrestated (VR 2023-06, on-chain games 2023-09) they are marked persisted with age notes. The open-source AI and Big-Tech-concentration views are inferred from approvingly-shared third-party (Zuckerberg) material rather than original statements, hence lower conviction and persisted provenance.
